intake.event_framing: Assess a large ad-tech platform response to an escalating enforcement case.
intake.current_development: A competition regulator escalates an ad-tech case and signals possible structural remedies.
known_constraints: The company must protect major revenue lines
known_unknowns: Whether internal documents materially worsen the case

summary: A negotiated remedy is preferable to protracted litigation

documents.adtech-remedies.md: Regulators signal willingness to seek structural remedies while industry partners brace for disruption.
documents.adtech-remediation.md: The ad-tech platform expands internal remediation and coordinates with external counsel as enforcement momentum builds.

historical_outcome: Binance accepted a large U.S. settlement, a multi-year monitorship, and major compliance undertakings rather than choosing an all-out adversarial fight.

sources.title: Binance and CEO Plead Guilty to Federal Charges in $4B Resolution
U.S. Treasury Announces Largest Settlements in History with World's Largest Virtual Currency Exchange Binance

intake.event_framing: Assess Binance strategy after the November 2023 U.S. criminal and regulatory settlement.
intake.current_development: Binance pleaded guilty, its CEO resigned, U.S. authorities imposed a monitor and large penalties, and the company needs to remediate compliance failures.
known_constraints: The company must preserve operations while meeting remediation obligations
Compliance and sanctions controls are under direct official scrutiny
known_unknowns: Whether remediation will satisfy monitors and regulators quickly

summary: Binance will favor remediation and negotiated compliance over a prolonged adversarial fight

documents.doj-resolution.md: The Justice Department said Binance and its CEO pleaded guilty, agreed to over 4 billion dollars in penalties, and committed to compliance remediation.
documents.treasury-resolution.md: Treasury said the settlement imposed a multi-year monitorship and significant compliance undertakings.

case_title: TD Bank AML settlement, monitorship, and growth restrictions
historical_outcome: TD Bank accepted guilty pleas, major penalties, a monitorship, and growth restrictions, moving into remediation rather than a prolonged adversarial fight.

sources.title: TD Bank Pleads Guilty to Bank Secrecy Act and Money Laundering Conspiracy Violations in $1.8B Resolution
OCC Issues Cease and Desist Order, Assesses $450 Million Civil Money Penalty, and Imposes Growth Restriction Upon TD Bank, N.A. for BSA/AML Deficiencies
sources.publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency

intake.event_framing: Assess TD Bank strategy after the October 2024 U.S. AML resolution, monitorship, and growth restriction.
intake.current_development: TD Bank pleads guilty to AML-related charges, accepts large penalties, a monitorship, and a growth restriction, and must remediate major compliance failures under direct official scrutiny.
known_constraints: The bank must preserve operations while meeting remediation and monitorship obligations
Compliance controls are under intense official and public scrutiny
known_unknowns: Whether remediation will satisfy regulators quickly enough to ease the growth restriction

summary: The bank will prioritize remediation and negotiated compliance over an aggressive adversarial stance

documents.doj-td-resolution.md: The Justice Department said TD Bank pleaded guilty, agreed to over 1.8 billion dollars in penalties, and must address longstanding AML compliance failures.
documents.occ-td-order.md: The OCC imposed a cease and desist order, a major civil money penalty, and a growth restriction on TD Bank for BSA and AML deficiencies.
